# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# Define the cluster name 
cluster_name: elasticsearch-logs

# Setup Elasticsearch node names and hostnames (replace with your actual values)
node.name: es01   # Node Name, e.g., 'es02', or just use default if you want an IP address in the range 192.168.*.'N' to avoid conflicts with other nodes running on a different port (default is randomly chosen by ES)
node.master: true    # Set master=true for cluster mode, else set it false and let es0x be part of your ring if you want the node(s). Default False  
network.host: 192.168.*     # Bind to all interfaces (default is 'localhost') unless a specific address is provided here e.g., bind_to = "0" or use default for random IP, else specify an IPv4/IPv6 Address in the range of your network
http.port: 9201   # Default port used by Elasticsearch (default = 9200) but can be configured to a different number if required e.g., '80' or any other available ports, default is randomly chosen and will change on each startup unless specified here otherwise
discovery.seed_hosts: ["192.168.*"]   # List of hostnames for discovery in the cluster (default = all hosts running locally) e.g., 'es03','localhost' or a comma-separated list, default is randomly chosen and will change on each startup unless specified here otherwise
cluster.initial_master_nodes: es01    # The name of your initial master node(s), if you want this cluster to be initially set up as one primary (default = None) e.g., 'es02' or a comma-separated list, default is randomly chosen and will change on each startup unless specified here otherwise
